content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

August 25, 1997

Paul A. Novak
Novak + Associates
108 North Brand Boulevard, Suite 201
Glendale, California 91203-2602

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-97-365

Dear Mr. Novak:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This request is being treated as a request for
informal advice pursuant to regulation 18329(b)(2)(B).²

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that
may have already taken place. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC
Ops. 71.)

QUESTIONS

1) As a commissioner of the Board of Zoning Adjustments, may you testify before a staff-
level decision-maker (i.e., zoning administrator, hearing officer) of the Glendale Planning
Division? Will you contact staff on the Glendale Planning Division with respect to pending
entitlement approvals filed by your firm on behalf of its clients?

2) May employees of your real estate consulting firm testify before a staff-level decision-
maker (i.e., zoning administrator, hearing officer) of the Glendale Planning Division? May
employees of your firm contact staff of the Glendale Planning Division with respect to pending
entitlement approvals filed by your firm on behalf of its clients?

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5560

File No. I-97-365
Page 2

3) May you testify before the Glendale City Council? May you contact city staff with
respect to pending entitlement approvals filed by your firm on behalf of its clients?

CONCLUSIONS

1) You may not testify before or contact staff on the Glendale Planning Division if the
Glendale Planning Division is appointed by or subject to the budgetary control of the Board of
Zoning Adjustments. You may not testify before or contact staff on the Glendale Planning
Division concerning a decision in which you have a financial interest, including pending
entitlement approvals filed by your firm on behalf of its clients, if the decision may come before,
is appealable to, or may be reconsidered by the Board of Zoning Adjustments. If the Act does
not prohibit you from appearing before the Glendale Planning Division, you must state on the
record that you are not appearing in your official capacity.

2) The Act does not prohibit employees of your firm from testifying before or contacting
staff on the Glendale Planning Division.

3) You may not testify before or contact staff on the Glendale City Council concerning a
decision in which you have a financial interest, including pending entitlement approvals filed by
your firm on behalf of its clients, if the decision may come before, is appealable to, or may be
reconsidered by the Board of Zoning Adjustments. If the Act does not prohibit you from
appearing before the Glendale City Council, you must state on the record that you are not
appearing in your official capacity.

FACTS

You were appointed to the Board of Zoning Adjustments (BZA) by the Glendale City
Council on September 26, 1995. Since then you have served on the BZA as an unpaid
commissioner. You are also the sole proprietor of Novak + Associates, a real estate consulting
firm established in January of 1993. The firm represents individuals, real estate developers, and
corporate and institutional clients seeking entitlement approvals (i.e., variances, conditional use
permits, and subdivisions) from cities and counties throughout the greater Los Angeles area. In
addition to you, the firm has two professional employees and hires contractors on a project-by-
project basis.

The city attorney of Glendale advised you that the Act prohibits you from testifying for a
client before the BZA, but that it does not prohibit employees of your firm from doing so. He
also advised you that the Act permits you to testify on behalf of a client before the Glendale City
Council. You would like to know whether the Act permits you to appear before the Glendale
Planning Division on behalf of a client.

File No. I-97-365
Page 3

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official from making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest.

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of section 87100 if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official or on any of the following:

"(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or
indirect investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

* * *

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a
commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating two hundred and fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by or promised to the public official within
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of
management." (Section 87103(a),(c),(d).)

Pursuant to section 87103(c), your real estate consulting firm and the clients of your firm
may be sources of income to you of $250 or more within the previous 12 months. In addition,
you have an investment interest in your firm that may be worth $1000 or more. (Section
87103(a).) As sole proprietor of your firm, you have a financial interest in your firm under
section 87103(d). Accordingly, you may not make, participate in making, or in any way attempt
to use your official position to influence a governmental decision if that decision will have a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your firm or its clients.

1. Board of Zoning Adjustments

Pursuant to regulation 18700.1(a) (copy enclosed), you may not appear before your own
agency³ on behalf of any client. In addition, you may not contact or otherwise attempt to
influence any member, officer, employee or consultant of your agency on behalf of any client.

³ The Commission has generally treated each public body, even within a single city, as separate public
bodies for purposes of regulation 18700.1; therefore, the Glendale City Council is not your agency. (Jeffrey Advice
Letter, No. A-92-610.)

File No. I-97-365
Page 4

However, the Act does not prohibit an employee of your real estate consulting firm from
representing a client before the BZA. (Wallace Advice Letter, No. A-95-120, copy enclosed.)
This would be the case even where the employee uses your firm's letterhead, which includes
your name. (Rumansoff Advice Letter, No. I-94-045.) You are, however, prohibited from
communicating with your fellow commissioners and staff regarding the matter and you are
disqualified from participating in the decision. In addition, you may not participate in any
decision that has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your economic interests.
(Section 87103.)

2. Glendale Planning Division

You have not provided any facts describing the Glendale Planning Division and its
relationship with BZA. As indicated above, regulation 18700.1(a) prohibits you from appearing
before your own agency. It also prohibits you from appearing before any agency appointed by or
subject to the budgetary control of your agency. In addition, regulation 18700.1(c) provides that
with regard to a governmental decision of an agency other than your agency, and other than an
agency appointed by or subject to the budgetary control of your agency, you are attempting to
use your official position to influence a decision if, for the purpose of influencing the decision,
you act or purport to act on behalf of, or as the representative of your agency to any member,
officer, employee or consultant of the other agency. Such actions include, but are not limited to
the use of official stationery.

Accordingly, you may not testify before or contact any member of the Glendale Planning
Division if it is part of your agency or if it is appointed by or subject to the budgetary control of
your agency. Moreover, under regulation 18700.1(a) as long as a governmental decision is
within or before your agency, you may not attempt to use your official position to influence that
decision. Thus, you may not testify before or contact a member, employee, or consultant of the
Glendale Planning Division with respect to a governmental decision in which you have a financial
interest, including pending entitlement approvals filed by your firm on behalf of its clients, if the
matter may come before, is appealable to, or may be reconsidered by the BZA. (Lyions Advice
Letter, No. A-94-013, copy enclosed.)

If the Act does not prohibit you from appearing before the Glendale Planning Division, you
must state on the record that you are not appearing in your official capacity. (Lyions, supra.)
The Act does not prohibit an employee of your firm from testifying before or contacting a
member of the Glendale Planning Division. (Wallace, supra.)

3. City Council

The city attorney advised you that you may personally appear before the Glendale City
Council on behalf of a client. As previously mentioned, as long as a governmental decision is
within or before your agency, you may not attempt to use your official position to influence that
decision. (Regulation 18700.1(a).) Thus, you may not testify before or contact a member,
employee or consultant of the Glendale City Council concerning a governmental decision in which

File No. I-97-365
Page 5

you have a financial interest, including a pending entitlement approval filed by your firm on
behalf of its clients, if the matter may come before, is appealable to, or may be reconsidered by
the BZA. (Lyons, supra.)

If the Act does not prohibit you from appearing before the Glendale City Council, you must
state on the record that you are not appearing in your official capacity. The Act does not prohibit
the employees of your firm from appearing before or contacting a member of the city council.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell

By: Julia Butcher
Graduate Legal Assistant, Legal Division
